L1.area: CGI and FastCGI protocol data parsing and record processing

L2.description: A buffer is allocated with a specified capacity and then unsafely marked as having its full length without initializing its contents. This uninitialized buffer is subsequently passed to a user-provided reader implementation. When the reader processes this buffer, it may read from uninitialized memory, leading to undefined behavior and potential information disclosure.

L3.area: CGI and FastCGI protocol data parsing and record processing
L3.description: A buffer is allocated with a specified capacity and then unsafely marked as having its full length without initializing its contents. This uninitialized buffer is subsequently passed to a user-provided reader implementation. When the reader processes this buffer, it may read from uninitialized memory, leading to undefined behavior and potential information disclosure.

ground_truth.reason: The key-value pair iterator creates uninitialized memory buffers using Vec::with_capacity followed by an unsafe set_len, then passes them to a user-provided Read implementation, exposing uninitialized heap memory.
